title: impatiens jurpia var. ravikumariana
intro: Impatiens jurpia var. ravikumariana, also known as 'Kerala Balsam', is a plant species belonging to the balsam family and is native to the Western Ghats of India. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving joint pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. In Ayurvedic herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and ointments that can be applied topically or consumed orally for medicinal purposes.
